title: Protocol for an in vivo CRISPR screen for germinal center B cells in mice using ecotropic retrovirus
author: Hong, Nussenzweig
description: SummaryThe lack of an in vitro model that recapitulates germinal center (GC) B cell biology necessitates the use of animal models for genetic studies. Here, we present a protocol for an in vivo CRISPR screen for GC B cells in mice using an ecotropic retrovirus. We describe steps for constructing a single guide (sgRNA) library and performing a genetic screen in a mouse model of protein immunization, including procedures for sequencing and data analysis.

steps_raw.title: Before you begin
steps_raw.instruction: This protocol is optimized for in vivo CRISPR screening in mouse GC B cells.

steps_raw.title: Ecotropic retrovirus preparation using Platinum-E packaging cell line
Ecotropic retrovirus preparation using Platinum-E packaging cell line
steps_raw.instruction: It is important for an effective CRISPR screening to ensure that your virus titer allows decent transduction efficiency. Performing an optimization experiment using GeneJuice Transfection Reagent is recommended to account for user-to-user variables. On the other hand, ensuring delivery of a single vector copy per cell is critical. The target MOI is 0.25 (âŒ20% infected population), where no more than 3% of the cell population is infected with two or more viral particles. For a full-scale library transduction, the user should calculate the required number of transduced cells for optimal library representation (500 â 1,000Ă coverage6). For example, to establish 1,000Ă coverage for 1,000 sgRNA library, 106 cells need to be transduced. With 20% transduction efficiency, a total of 5 Ă 106 cells should be used. Additionally, to account for the engrafting efficiency in the recipient animals, we recommend the user to use at least four times the number of cells calculated above.4.At day â1, seed 0.3 Ă 106 Plat-E cells per well in a 6-well plate in 3 mL cell culture medium without antibiotics.5.At day 0, follow the manufacturerâs instruction for GeneJuice Transfection Reagent. Use 2 ÎŒg pooled sgRNA library and 3 ÎŒL transfection reagent in 100 ÎŒL Opti-MEM medium per well.CRITICAL: Slowly dispense the DNA and the reagent while keeping the pipette tip at least 2 mm below the surface of the liquid inside the Eppendorf tube and changing the tipâs positions every second (Figure 2).

steps_raw.title: Ecotropic retrovirus preparation using Platinum-E packaging cell line
steps_raw.instruction:
6.At day 2, collect supernatant and freeze at â80 Â°C.
Note: Freeze-thaw cycles do not significantly decrease the transduction efficiency at least up to 3 cycles (Figure 3). Viral concentration step is not necessary. This protocol does not require usage of polybrene.
Figure 2An illustration of dispensing the transfection reagent and the plasmids in the medium

steps_raw.title: Ecotropic retrovirus preparation using Platinum-E packaging cell line
steps_raw.instruction:
Optional: Filter the supernatant through 0.45 ÎŒm polyethersulfone filter before freezing.
Figure 3The effects of freeze-thaw cycles on transduction efficiency

steps_raw.title: Spleen harvesting and genomic DNA extraction from an in vivo screen
Spleen harvesting and genomic DNA extraction from an in vivo screen
steps_raw.instruction: Process the spleens and sort for the pooled sgRNA library-transduced GC B cells before gDNA extraction. B cell isolation protocol is modified from steps 5â7 in step-by-step method details âtransduction with pooled sgRNA library retrovirusâ to enrich for GC B cells to shorten the sorting time.21.At Day 10 after NP-OVA immunization, euthanize mice according to institutional guidelines.22.Harvest the spleens from the recipient mice.a.Mince the spleens and pass through a 70 ÎŒm cell strainer to generate single cell splenocyte suspensions.Note: Usage of enzymes is not necessary.b.Count the cells.Note: The typical range of the number of cells is 0.3â1 Ă 108 cells per spleen.23.Spin down the cells at 300 Ă g at 4 Â°C for 5 min. Resuspend 1 Ă 108 cells/1 mL FACS buffer.24.Add 1 ÎŒg anti-IgD mAb and 1 ÎŒg anti-CD38 mAb per 1 mL cell suspension. Incubate at 4 Â°C for 15 min while rotating the tubes.25.Wash the cells with 10 mL FACS buffer. Spin down at 300 Ă g at 4 Â°C for 5 min.26.Resuspend 1 Ă 108 cells/700 ÎŒL FACS buffer.27.Add 100 ÎŒL each of CD43, Ter-119, and Streptavidin microbeads per 700 ÎŒL cell suspension. Incubate at 4 Â°C for 15 min while rotating the tubes.28.Wash the cells with 10 mL FACS buffer. Spin down at 300 Ă g at 4 Â°C for 5 min.29.Isolate GC B cells following the manufacturerâs instructions for Miltenyi microbeads.30.Spin down the cells at 300 Ă g at 4 Â°C for 10 min.a.Resuspend 1 Ă 108 cells/200 ÎŒL FACS buffer containing Fc-blocking antibody (anti-CD16/CD32 mAb) at 1 ÎŒg/mL.b.Incubate at 4 Â°C for 15 min.31.Prepare antibody staining mixture containing Zombie NIR live/dead dye at 1:250 dilution and anti-B220, anti-CD38, and anti-CD95 mAb all at 2 ÎŒg/mL.a.Add 200 ÎŒL staining mixture/200 ÎŒL cell suspension.b.Incubate at 4 Â°C for 15 min.32.Wash the cells with 10 mL FACS buffer.33.Sort for mTagBFP2+ GC B cells (live/dead-, B220+, CD38-, CD95+) at 10,000 events/sec using 70 ÎŒm nozzle (BD FACS Aria II) in 1.5-mL Eppendorf tubes containing 300 l PBS (Figure 7). Spin down at 300 Ă g at 4 Â°C for 5 min.Figure 7Gating strategy for sorting transduced GC B cellsAmong live/dead-, B220+ B cells, GC B cells were gated as CD38-, CD95+ cells.GFP+, mTagBFP2+ GC B cells were sorted.CRITICAL: For 1,000 sgRNA library, the user is recommended to collect at least 5 Ă 105 cells per replicate for the total of at least 3â4 replicates. Each replicate is pooled from 20 mice.Pause point: After spinning down and removing the supernatant, the cell pellets can be stored at â80 Â°C until the user is ready to extract gDNA.34.Remove the frozen input and output sample pellets from the â80 Â°C freezer and thaw at 20â25 Â°C.35.Extract gDNA from cell pellets using QIAamp DNA Blood Mini Kits following the manufacturerâs instructions.a.Add 4 ÎŒL RNase A stock solution (100 mg/mL)/200 ÎŒL PBS with 106 cells before starting the gDNA extraction.b.Load up to 106 cells per column.c.During the elution step, incubate the columns loaded with 50 ÎŒL nuclease-free water at 20â25 Â°C for 5 min before centrifugation. Repeat elution once more and combine with the first elution.Note: It is recommended to concentrate the eluate by ethanol precipitation before performing PCR amplification.36.Add 2 ÎŒL Pellet Paint to the eluate followed by 0.1 volume of 3 M sodium acetate. Mix by vortexing.37.Add 2 volume of ethanol and vortex. Incubate at 20â25 Â°C for 2 min.38.Spin down at 16,000 Ă g at 4 Â°C for 5 min. Carefully remove supernatant.39.Wash with 70% ethanol and vortex.40.Repeat step 38. Wash with 100% ethanol and vortex.41.Repeat step 38. Air-dry residual ethanol and dissolve the pellet with 12 ÎŒL nuclease-free water for up to 106 sorted cells.42.Measure the concentration of gDNA using Nanodrop or Qubit machine.CRITICAL: The total yield of gDNA should be at least 100 â 500 ng per 105 cells. See âtroubleshooting.âPause point: gDNA can be stored at 4 or â80 Â°C.

steps_raw.title: Sequencing and data analysis from an in vivo screen
Sequencing and data analysis from an in vivo screen
steps_raw.instruction: PCR amplify sgRNA sequences from gDNA for high-throughput sequencing and analysis.43.Prepare PCR master mix.ReagentFinal concentrationAmount5Ă Phusion Green HF buffer1Ă4 ÎŒLdNTP (1 mM)200 ÎŒM4 ÎŒLForward primer (5 ÎŒM)500 nM2 ÎŒLReverse primer (5 ÎŒM)500 nM2 ÎŒLDMSOâ0.6 ÎŒLgDNAâ7.2 ÎŒLPhusion Hot Start II (2 U/ÎŒL)0.02 U/ÎŒL0.2 ÎŒL

steps_raw.title: Sequencing and data analysis from an in vivo screen
steps_raw.instruction:
CRITICAL: For 1,000 sgRNA library, the user is recommended to use at least 500 ng for downstream PCR amplification.
StepsTemperatureTimeCyclesInitial Denaturation98Â°C1 min1Denaturation98Â°C10 s33 cyclesAnnealing62.5Â°C30 sâExtension72Â°C15 sâFinal extension72Â°C5 min1Hold4Â°Cforeverforever

steps_raw.title: Sequencing and data analysis from an in vivo screen
steps_raw.instruction:
44.Run PCR reaction mixture on 2% agarose gel with DNA ladder.45.Excise the band at 500 bp for DNA purification by gel extraction, following the manufacturerâs instructions. Non-specific bands are not typically observed.46.Measure the concentration of PCR product using Nanodrop or Qubit machine. The expected amount of PCR product is approximately 300â1,000 ng.47.Sequence the libraries on an Illumina sequencer. For a library size of 1,000 sgRNAs, we performed 33 cycles of PCR and sequenced with a depth of 400 million total reads.48.Use Geneious or other equivalent programs to map the reads to the reference sgRNA library sequences. Create counts table by exporting documents.49.Use MAGeCK to calculate statistical enrichment and depletion of the genes in the screen by providing the counts table as input.
Optional: Perform normalization using --control-sgrna option.
